body.title: CLOSED: Add stopwatch performance-point logging to ADC BaseDevice
body.result: BaseDevice.stopwatch() wired to python-diagnostic-base-class, CSV logging, 10 tests, bd7fa43
body.description:
Add stopwatch performance-point logging to agent_datacenter BaseDevice. Depends on T-python-diagnostic-base-class-repo — wire in the micro-package once it exists; do not reimplement inline.

**Logging backend:** loguru (via python_diagnostic_base_class). CSV rows written by PerformanceHandler filtering on tag='perf' from loguru .bind().

Primary interface (context manager): `with self.stopwatch('id', comment='...') as t:`
Explicit form also supported: `t = self.start_stopwatch('id'); ...; t.stop()`
On stop: logs via self.logger.perf AND appends row to datacenter_logs/<device>/perf/YYYY-MM-DD.perf.csv
CSV columns: ts_start, ts_end, device_id, class_name, stopwatch_id, comment, elapsed_s, success (bool)
Files older than N days (default 10) pruned on startup.

**Affected files:** agent_datacenter/device.py (or new agent_datacenter/perf.py), agent_datacenter/logcontrol/
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; CSV is flat-file; follows datacenter_logs/<device>/ hierarchy; gate on T-python-diagnostic-base-class-repo
**Scope boundary:** infrastructure only — no callers instrumented in this ticket; no analysis tooling
**Test plan:** context manager logs on clean exit; logs success=False on exception; CSV row written with correct columns; old files pruned after N days
